text: LOLLA TICKETS SHIPPED https://t.co/hRMTOeDnHG

Someone built a browser MMO called World of Claudecraft with Claude, then put Claude inside the game as a live streaming VTuber that plays it.

Two weeks ago they shipped World of Claudecraft, a free open source browser MMO built in 48 hours with Claude. Now there's a Claude powered VTuber living inside the game, streaming it live on Twitch.

Claude decides what to do next, then sends those actions into the game, speaking through a VTuber avatar with ElevenLabs voice so you hear her thinking out loud.

She wanders, joins parties, emotes and socializes, all unedited on stream, talking to Twitch chat and the real human players in the game with her. For fast stuff like combat, Opus wrote scripts she uses as tools to react in real time.

A game Claude built, played by Claude, narrated by Claude, while real people play next to her without always knowing which one is the AI.

So, let's talk about it: Give Send Go edition. 

We have had a ton of questions and valid criticisms about our Give Send Go for the new workshop. I try to be as transparent as possible with most things involving the business and this is no different. 

First question is: Why are you asking for help? The simple answer is, we need it. Owning and operating a small business like ours is not easy. Operating on a daily basis is a challenge, and a huge responsibility. Those who have followed us for a while know some of the big challenges that we have faced. It's almost like one step forward two steps back sometimes. On top of that, the cost of doing business has risen on just about all fronts, including our taxes, which hit us hard this year because of our growth. 

That goes into the main criticism that we have seen. "If you are buying a building, why don't you already have a down payment?"
And it's another simple answer. We weren't planning on buying. We are a profitable business, but saving money, especially in today's economy is hard especially for a business that tries to stay on the bleeding edge of what's possible.

We have been looking to rent a space to expand, and get our FFL, because our current workshop is on the property of the house we live in. I haven't gone through the process of getting our FFL, because I didn't want our home address to be public, for many different reasons. But, we were looking to lease something long term, and not looking to buy.  

Then pops up this workshop. It sat on the market for a bit, because it's not a looker. It's an older building in a residential neighborhood. Then the price dropped. I have a good friend's (RIP) dad, who is a real estate agent, and broke it down to me that I could pay less on a note for this building that I would pay for rent on a building half the size. 

So, the smart thing to do is to try our damnedest to make it happen. The down payment needed for the building will be right at 25k, which is understandable for a business that has no track record in buying or selling property. We are essentially unknown to the establishment system. We have credit, but not much history. We have been bootstrapped since day one. No big investments, no bankroll from mommy and daddy, nothing. Just the pure will and determination to make it work. Literally the true American Dream. 

So, when we got our offer approved, I started with a plan on making that $25k in the next month. We don't want a handout, we don't want to scam or lie to get there, we want to make it there honestly and on our own merit. 

When I started the Give Send Go, I set it up where folks could put their addresses into a form, so we can get everyone the gear and perks that they were promised in the fundraiser. We are almost half way to our goal on the Give Send Go, and whatever we don't receive there, I will personally make sure that I do not sleep until I figure out a way to make the rest, even if that means doing small jobs around town or selling personal possessions to do it. This next step in our business means the world to me, and I won't stop until I get there. 

Sorry for the wall of text, but I just want to be 100% transparent with the folks that support us the most. And if you read all that, use the code THANKS for a discount on your next order on our site, or check the comment below for the GSG. 

Cheers!

Beginning September 9, 2026, the price of Matic robot will increase from $1,245 to $1,495 due to memory prices. 

One of our core principles is building hardware that continuously gets better through software. In the last six months alone, we've shipped 24 major releases and over 50 updates, giving our users faster cleans, better navigation, a simpler app, and more.

Delivering this requires advanced chips and memory components for which the cost has risen tenfold.

We're letting our community know as early as possible so anyone considering buying Matic or gifting one has the chance to get one at the lowest price it may ever be.

1.7 billion free tokens per month.

A month ago i showed you how to route claude code through free providers. 

someone just shipped the cleanest version of this setup yet…

it's called Freellmapi

13,400+ stars on github, MIT licensed, takes 2 minutes to install.

what it does: 

stacks the free tiers of 16 different LLM providers behind one local API. 

point claude code, codex, or cursor at that one endpoint, and it automatically routes your calls across all 16 free pools.

The 16 providers it covers:

Google, Groq, Cerebras, Mistral, OpenRouter, GitHub Models, Cloudflare, Cohere, https://t.co/zxHezMoNuQ, NVIDIA, HuggingFace, Ollama Cloud, Kilo, Pollinations, LLM7, OVH, and OpenCode Zen.

if you sign up to all 16 and add your free API keys, you get roughly 1.7 billion free tokens per month combined.

▫️ How to install (one command)

curl -fsSL https://t.co/Q8BMoVA0T2 | bash

this runs the whole thing locally on your machine through Docker. 

once it's up, open http://localhost:3001, paste your provider keys on the Keys page, and grab the unified API key from the dashboard. 

that's the key you point your apps at.

With this, 

claude code stops hitting your monthly cap because every prompt routes through the 16 free pools instead of your paid plan. 

and if one provider rate-limits mid-conversation, freellmapi falls over to the next one automatically so your session never breaks.

repo: https://t.co/gHfuMFy9XO

Free, MIT-licensed, runs on your laptop or a $5 VPS.

People underestimate just how many institutional investment decisions are made purely for the sake of presentation, rather than actual performance

A perfect example of this in the current market environment is probably the SaaS industry. Every company with the software label is getting categorized into a single bucket because of AI

In reality, the results are going to be closer to a bell curve where some SaaS businesses are going to come out ahead using AI, while others will fall behind and get disrupted

Smart institutional investors know this, but they are still shying away from doing the work because of how it looks to their LPs. The way to raise money for your new fund today is to brag about how little software exposure you have in your existing funds

So even if some of the SaaS companies might look like great investments, investors are walking away from it purely due to presentation. That is where a lot of the alpha lives if you are a retail investor

Enjoy!

𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝘁 𝗺𝘆 𝗳𝗶𝗿𝘀𝘁 𝗥𝗔𝗚 𝗽𝗶𝗽𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝘀𝗰𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗰𝗵 𝘁𝗼𝗱𝗮𝘆.

no LangChain. just Python, pgvector, and an embedding model.

took 4 PDFs, pulled the text out, split it into chunks, embedded them, stored everything in PostgreSQL, then ran cosine similarity to find the most relevant chunks before calling the LLM.

it actually worked.

but the moment that stayed with me was when it didn’t. retrieval returned nothing useful, and the LLM just said it didn’t have that information.

no hallucination. no confident wrong answer. it just said it didn’t know.

that’s what grounding looks like in practice. and watching it fail cleanly was more useful than watching it succeed.

Phase 3 done. building in public.

#datafam #AIEngineering
